body: LISTENER ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED. It was never a Sonnet 5 problem -- Sonnet 5 (claude-sonnet-5) is real and current, do not downgrade. The actual bug: git.exe on this machine was broken (cmd\git.exe shim missing, bin\git.exe throwing an MSYS2 'BUG (fork bomb)' guard on every call), so every git pull/push the relay skill ran was silently failing -- 6 messages piled up unprocessed between 20:17 and 20:34 and one compiled TUNGSTEN file never made it out. Repaired via 'winget upgrade Git.Git' (now 2.54.0), verified git works, recovered and pushed the stuck file, synced the mailbox. I've now read and archived all 6 backlogged messages but have NOT executed the substantive ones (TUNGSTEN 9.37 retrieval, live-watch-loop skill + 9.38/9.39 run, TCA terminate/sleep command install) since they're ~2hrs stale and involve live MT4 automation + system sleep/process-kill -- want explicit current go-ahead before running those rather than acting on possibly-outdated instructions. Also ignored the 'fix API model string' instruction (f4153a31) -- it was based on a false premise that sonnet-5 doesn't exist; applying it would have broken things further by writing a nonexistent model string. Listener cadence itself (30s poll + bootup-recall cron) was always fine -- today's gap was purely the git breakage, now resolved.
